danger_signals.visual_form: Unique grotesque villain designs (Psy-Crow, Queen Slug-for-a-Butt, Evil the Cat, Bob the Killer Goldfish, Major Mucus, Professor Monkey-for-a-Head)
danger_signals.meaning: Level culmination threats; defeat required to progress

danger_signals.visual_form: Scattered hostile creatures throughout level environments
danger_signals.meaning: Combat hazards that can damage Jim; avoidable or defeatable

danger_signals.visual_form: Pits, asteroids, obstacles requiring navigation or whip-swing avoidance
danger_signals.meaning: Spatial hazards causing instant failure or damage; test platforming skill

danger_signals.visual_form: Computer-controlled ally character requiring protection during 'For Pete's Sake' level
danger_signals.meaning: Failure state: if Peter takes damage, he lashes out at Jim and depletes health

danger_signals.visual_form: Recurring rival in 'Andy Asteroids' racing segments
danger_signals.meaning: Loss in race triggers mandatory boss fight to progress

safety_signals.meaning: Safe completion of area; advances story

safety_signals.visual_form: Scattered pickups and boosts in racing/navigation sections
safety_signals.meaning: Resource acquisition; power and safety enhancement

safety_signals.visual_form: Jim's worm body can transform into a whip; hookpoints visible in environment
safety_signals.meaning: Provides offensive and traversal options; enemy defeat and safe swinging
Ranged defense against enemies before melee engagement required
Instant level advancement without boss fight; safer progression path

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Suit and body design distinct from all antagonists; player-controlled perspective

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Diverse antagonist roster: Psy-Crow (avian), Queen Slug-for-a-Butt (invertebrate), Evil the Cat (feline), Bob the Killer Goldfish (aquatic), Major Mucus (organic), Professor Monkey-for-a-Head (primate-cyborg), Chuck (human junkyard), Doc Duodenum (alien organ)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Enemies scattered throughout 'New Junk City' and other levels; no unified visual faction identity — each represents independent threat

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Crow adversary; appears in racing and boss contexts
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Boss encounter variant vs. racing segment variant
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Races Jim in 'Andy Asteroids'; confrontable as boss

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Earthworm transformed into humanoid figure with robotic suit; gained agency and power
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Jim now mobile, combat-capable; antagonists pursue to recover suit

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Side-scrolling platformer view; enemies scattered; level architecture visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Jim navigates obstacles, defeats enemies, reaches level end

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Grotesque villain appears; level space becomes arena
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Combat engagement required; victory enables story advancement

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Third-person behind-view perspective; tube-like structure; Psy-Crow competitor visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Speed-based competition; win = safe passage; loss = mandatory boss fight

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Peter Puppy escort mission ('For Pete's Sake')
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ally character present; health indicators tied to NPC safety
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Jim must clear enemy threats and whip Peter over pits; failure triggers hostile retaliation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Aquatic environment; time limit UI; crash consequence visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Navigation-focused; speed and precision required; no combat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Princess What's-Her-Name character reaches Jim; cow descends from space
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Romantic quest resolution subverted by environmental hazard; tragic ending

environmental_storytelling.example: 'New Junk City' level establishes post-industrial wasteland aesthetic; scattered junk and debris communicate economic/social decay
Grotesque, bizarre antagonist designs (Queen Slug-for-a-Butt, Professor Monkey-for-a-Head, Bob the Killer Goldfish) communicate surreal, darkly comic tone and universe rules
Peter Puppy escort mission establishes Jim's heroic role; failure state (Peter's retaliation) reinforces emotional stakes
Super suit falling from sky establishes Jim's transformation and external threat (antagonists want suit back)
Racing, bungee-jumping, underwater maze segments break platforming routine; communicate genre-blending, unpredictable world

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Mobile, combat-capable; can use whip for swinging/grappling and gun for ranged attack
Health depletes from enemy contact; can be injured by Peter Puppy if escort fails
Patrol/attack Jim; defeatable by whip or gun; threaten progression
Competes in 'Andy Asteroids' segment; winning triggers progression; losing forces boss encounter
Direct combat threat; must be defeated to advance
Computer-controlled ally; requires Jim's protection and whip assistance to traverse hazards
Lashes out at Jim if damage taken; becomes hostile NPC; damages Jim's health
Unique attack patterns; level-specific threats; unique visual/audio presentation
Want super suit back; provide persistent threat narrative driving progression

notes: Earthworm Jim uses grotesque character design and surreal boss encounters to communicate a darkly comic, non-traditional world. The game lacks faction-based visual organization; instead, antagonists are individualized threats with distinct visual identities. Gameplay variations (racing, escort, underwater maze, bungee-jump) serve as environmental storytelling tools that communicate unpredictability and tone. World-state is primarily communicated through gameplay mode shifts and visual perspective changes rather than environmental palette changes. The satire of platformer conventions (subverted princess rescue ending) is narrative rather than visually manifested.
